MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
T. Leslie S.(No phone number at this time) for Plaintiff in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - ARGUED. The Court inquires why it should vacate Judge Jackson's ruling. Plaintiff does not waive the Court Reporter and requests a 40 day continuance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF T. LESLIE S.C.'S Motion For Reconsideration of the order filed May 2, 2017 dismissing this case is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show new or different facts, law or circumstances that warrant vacating the May 2 order. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Fourth Amended Complaint For Failure To Comply With Court Order, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Strike
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff T. Leslie S.C. in Propria Persona.
Nahal Metanat of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP telephone number 415-278-9400 for Defendant Christopher Fromer.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER FROMER'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Fourth Amended Complaint For Failure To Comply With Court Order, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Strike is denied. While plaintiff's failure to comply with the court order is troubling and plaintiff is admonished that she must strictly comply with all court orders and rules including giving notice of ex parte applications, Mr. Fromer has not shown that he is prejudiced by the late filing of the fourth amended complaint and the relief he requests is inconsistent with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Order Sustaining Defendant Christopher Fromer'S Demurrer Without Leave To Amend
Off Calendar Mar-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff T LESLIE S appearing in pro per;
Atty. TODD A. ANGSTADT, ESQ. of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP, Tel No. 415-278-9400 appearing for Defendant Christopher Fromer.

Matter argued and submitted by parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF T LESLIE S' Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Order Sustaining Defendant Christopher Fromer's Demurrer Without Leave To Amend, Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of the oral order sustaining Fromer's demurrer without leave to amend is re-characterized as an invitation for this court to reconsider the earlier order and, as so re-characterized, it is granted and plaintiff is allowed twenty days to file a fourth amended complaint. While plaintiff has not satisfied the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 1008, the court has the authority to revisit interim rulings. (Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094). Judge Goldsmith is retired and not available to hear this motion, so it is being heard by Judge Kahn, the current law and motion judge. The interests of justice and the strong policy of deciding cases on their merits favors giving plaintiff another opportunity to amend her complaint. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
